engineIntelligence.h2: Mazda B Series Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Mazda B Series in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data for commercial pickup trucks.

cards.altText: Mazda B Series WL engine - diesel 2.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Injector pump wear - high-pressure pump degradation from 90k miles causes hard starting and power loss.
Turbocharger failure - WL-T units suffer boost loss and oil leaks at high mileage.

cards.altText: Mazda B Series MD25 engine - diesel 2.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Head gasket failure - coolant/oil mixing reported on high-mileage commercial use units.
Timing belt tensioner wear - belt slip risk if service intervals exceeded on fleet vehicles.

cards.altText: Mazda B Series F2 engine - petrol 2.2L replacement UK
knownFailures: Carburettor jet clogging - fuel delivery issues on early non-EFI units stored long-term.
Valve stem seal wear - oil consumption increases after 80k miles on higher-mileage examples.

engineGuide.h2: Mazda B Series Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mazda B Series across UF and UN generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK commercial vehicle specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.5L Diesel
entries.history: The WL became the backbone of the B Series diesel range, powering the popular B2500 workhorse pickup across two generations with proven reliability for commercial use.
compatibleVariants: B2500, B2500 Turbo, B2600, Double Cab, Single Cab, Fleet Spec
commonFailures: Injector pump wear - high-pressure pump degradation from 90k miles causes hard starting and reduced power.
Turbocharger oil seal failure - WL-T units develop boost leaks and smoke at high mileage.
Glow plug relay failure - cold-start difficulties reported on pre-2000 units.
entries.cta: Get quotes for WL Mazda B Series engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Turbo Diesel
entries.history: The turbocharged WL-T delivered increased torque for loaded commercial use, becoming the preferred choice for UK tradespeople and fleet operators.
compatibleVariants: B2500 Turbo, B2500 Intercooler, B2600 Turbo, Double Cab Turbo, 4x4 Turbo
commonFailures: Turbocharger bearing wear - boost pressure loss and oil consumption increase after 100k miles.
Intercooler hose splitting - common on high-mileage commercial vehicles with frequent heavy loads.
entries.cta: Get quotes for WL-T Mazda B Series engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Diesel (Ford-platform)
entries.history: The MD25, derived from Ford's diesel architecture, powered the UN-generation B Series with improved emissions compliance for post-2000 MoT requirements.
compatibleVariants: B2500 UN, B2600 UN, B2600 4x4 UN, Double Cab UN
commonFailures: Head gasket failure - coolant/oil mixing reported on high-mileage commercial use units.
Timing belt tensioner wear - belt slip risk if service intervals exceeded on fleet vehicles.
entries.cta: Get quotes for MD25 Mazda B Series engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Turbo Diesel (Ford-platform)
entries.history: The turbocharged MD25-T offered enhanced torque for the UN-generation B Series, popular with users requiring towing capacity and loaded performance.
compatibleVariants: B2500 Turbo UN, B2600 Turbo UN, Double Cab Turbo UN, 4x4 Turbo UN
commonFailures: Turbo actuator sticking - variable geometry mechanism clogs with carbon on short-journey commercial use.
EGR valve clogging - emissions system faults trigger warning lights on post-2000 units.
entries.cta: Get quotes for MD25-T Mazda B Series engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L Petrol
entries.history: The F2 powered the entry-level B2200 petrol variants, offering simple, carburetted reliability for light commercial and private pickup use.
compatibleVariants: B2200, B2200 4x4, Single Cab, Double Cab
commonFailures: Carburettor jet clogging - fuel delivery issues on early non-EFI units stored long-term.
Valve stem seal wear - oil consumption increases after 80k miles on higher-mileage examples.
entries.cta: Get quotes for F2 Mazda B Series engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The smaller-displacement FE offered improved fuel economy for urban commercial use, fitted to early B2200 variants before the F2 became standard.
compatibleVariants: B2200 FE, B2200 FE-DOHC, Early UF variants
commonFailures: Timing belt tensioner wear - belt replacement critical at 60k miles to prevent interference damage.
Distributor cap corrosion - misfires reported on vehicles exposed to coastal or winter road salt.
entries.cta: Get quotes for FE Mazda B Series engine replacement

entries.title: 2.6L Petrol
entries.history: The G6 delivered stronger performance for the B2600, popular with users requiring extra power for towing or 4x4 applications.
compatibleVariants: B2600, B2600 4x4, B2600 Double Cab, B2600 G6-EFI
commonFailures: Oil pump wear - low oil pressure warnings reported on high-mileage commercial use units.
Exhaust manifold cracking - heat cycling causes cracks on frequently loaded vehicles.
entries.cta: Get quotes for G6 Mazda B Series engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L V6 Petrol
entries.history: The JE V6 powered the range-topping B3000, offering smooth power for users prioritising refinement over maximum torque.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - rattling noise on cold start reported after 90k miles.
Intake manifold gasket leaks - vacuum leaks cause rough idle on higher-mileage units.
entries.cta: Get quotes for JE Mazda B Series engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Turbo Diesel
entries.history: The Ford-derived Duratorq TDCi brought common-rail injection to the late UN B Series, improving refinement and emissions for post-2004 compliance.
compatibleVariants: B2500 TDCi, B2500 UN late, B2600 TDCi
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump wear - hard starting and power loss reported on high-mileage commercial units.
DPF/oxidation catalyst clogging - emissions system faults on short-journey fleet vehicles.
entries.cta: Get quotes for Duratorq 2.5 Mazda B Series engine replacement

Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

commonProblems.h2: Mazda B Series Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mazda B Series is a robust commercial workhorse, but high-mileage diesel units (WL, WL-T, MD25) can suffer from injector pump wear, turbocharger failure, and head gasket issues. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the problem groups below to assess repair vs replacement costs for your B Series.

problems.h4: WL Injector Pump Failure - Why Mazda B Series Diesel Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Mazda B Series B2500, B2600 (1985-2006, WL & WL-T diesel engines)
problems.rootCause: High-pressure injector pump internal wear reduces fuel delivery pressure, causing hard starting, rough idle, and power loss under load.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Injector Pump Repair - Refurbish existing pump
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove pump, bench-test, replace worn plungers/seals, recalibrate
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early; may not resolve secondary damage

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Pump Replacement - Used/recon pump fit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Source compatible WL pump, fit, bleed fuel system, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective if engine block is sound; 12-month pump warranty typical

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Complete WL unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt WL engine with upgraded pump, full warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage B2500; eliminates multiple failure risks

problems.recommendation: On a B2500 worth under £3,000, a full rebuilt WL engine often offers better long-term value than repeated pump repairs. We recommend getting quotes for both options before deciding.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt WL engine prices for your Mazda B Series

problems.h4: WL-T Turbocharger Failure - Boost Loss & Smoke on Mazda B Series
problems.affectedModels: Mazda B Series B2500 Turbo, B2600 Turbo (1995-2006, WL-T & MD25-T engines)
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger bearing wear and oil seal degradation cause boost pressure loss, blue smoke from exhaust, and increased oil consumption.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Turbo Rebuild - Service existing turbo
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, replace bearings/seals, balance, reinstall
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if housing is undamaged; 6-month warranty typical

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Turbo Replacement - Recon turbo fit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Source compatible WL-T turbo, fit, test boost pressure
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable fix if engine internals are healthy

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Complete WL-T unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt turbo diesel engine with upgraded turbo
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage commercial B Series; comprehensive warranty

problems.recommendation: If your B2500 has over 120k miles and shows signs of turbo failure, budget for a full rebuilt WL-T engine - it addresses turbo, injector pump, and bottom-end wear in one solution.
problems.cta: Get quotes for WL-T turbo diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Head Gasket Failure - Coolant/Oil Mixing on Mazda B Series Diesel
problems.affectedModels: Mazda B Series B2500, B2600 (1998-2006, MD25 & MD25-T engines)
problems.rootCause: Cylinder head warping or gasket degradation allows coolant and oil to mix, causing overheating, white exhaust smoke, and potential engine seizure.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Gasket Replacement - Head skim & refit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, machine surface, fit new gasket, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if block/head are undamaged; risk of recurrence

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Complete MD25 unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt MD25 engine with upgraded gasket, full warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for commercial vehicles; eliminates recurrence risk

problems.recommendation: Head gasket failure on an MD25 often indicates broader cooling system stress. For a workhorse B Series, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components offers better reliability and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare MD25 rebuilt engine prices for your Mazda B Series

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Interference Damage Risk on Mazda B Series
problems.affectedModels: Mazda B Series B2200, B2500, B2600 (all belt-driven engines: F2, FE, G6, MD25)
problems.rootCause: Timing belt wear or tensioner failure causes belt slip or snap, leading to valve/piston collision on interference engines.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Belt Replacement - Before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace belt, tensioner, water pump; reset service interval

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - After belt failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine after valve/piston damage
repairOptions.longevity: Only option if belt has snapped on interference engine

problems.recommendation: If your B Series timing belt history is unknown, replace it immediately as preventative maintenance. If the belt has already failed, a rebuilt engine is typically the only viable repair.
problems.cta: Check timing belt service history for your Mazda B Series

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mazda B Series
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing injector pump wear, turbo failure, or head gasket issues, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mazda B Series specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes and get your commercial vehicle back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mazda B Series Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mazda B Series and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mazda B Series Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Mazda B Series is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

groups.title: Mazda B Series Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Mazda B Series 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.2L R2 diesel powered early B2200 variants (1985-1990), offering basic, naturally aspirated reliability for light commercial use before the WL family became standard.
commonFailurePoints: glow plug relay failure on high-mileage units
items.cta: Get quotes for Mazda B Series 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Mazda B Series 2.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.5L WL and MD25 diesel engines formed the backbone of the B Series range (1985-2006), powering the popular B2500 and B2600 with proven torque for commercial towing and loaded work.
commonFailurePoints: head gasket issues on high-mileage commercial use
items.cta: Get quotes for Mazda B Series 2.5L diesel engines

groups.title: Mazda B Series Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Mazda B Series 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L FE petrol engine offered improved fuel economy for urban commercial use on early B2200 variants, before the larger F2 became the standard petrol option.
commonFailurePoints: distributor cap corrosion on coastal/exposed vehicles
items.cta: Compare Mazda B Series 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mazda B Series 2.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.2L F2 petrol engine powered the majority of B2200 petrol variants (1985-1999), offering simple carburetted or early EFI reliability for light commercial and private pickup use.
commonFailurePoints: Carburettor jet clogging on early non-EFI units; valve stem seal wear after 80k miles
items.cta: Compare Mazda B Series 2.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mazda B Series 2.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.6L G6 petrol engine delivered stronger performance for the B2600, popular with users requiring extra power for towing, 4x4 applications or heavier payloads.
commonFailurePoints: Oil pump wear on high-mileage commercial units; exhaust manifold cracking from heat cycling
items.cta: Compare Mazda B Series 2.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mazda B Series 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0L JE V6 petrol engine powered the range-topping B3000 (1998-2006), offering smooth, refined power for users prioritising driving comfort over maximum torque.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner wear causing cold-start rattles; intake manifold gasket leaks on higher-mileage units
items.cta: Compare Mazda B Series 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mazda B Series 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.0L Ford-derived V6 powered the B4000 variant (2001-2006), sharing architecture with contemporary Ford Rangers for users requiring maximum petrol power.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain guide wear; coolant crossover gasket leaks on high-mileage examples
items.cta: Compare Mazda B Series 4.0L petrol engine prices

fuelTypes.h2: Mazda B Series Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Mazda B Series engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early R2 diesel to the latest Duratorq TDCi.

items.title: Mazda B Series Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel engines dominated the Mazda B Series range in the UK, with the 2.5L WL and MD25 families powering the popular B2500 and B2600 workhorse pickups across two generations.
items.descriptor: Diesel engines dominated the Mazda B Series range in the UK, with the 2.5L WL and MD25 families powering the popular B2500 and B2600 workhorse pickups across two generations.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mazda B Series Diesel Engine Families
families: WL - most common replacement engine (B2500/B2600), injector pump and turbo failure risks
WL-T - turbocharged variant, higher torque for loaded commercial use, boost-related failures
MD25 - Ford-platform derivative (UN generation), head gasket and timing belt concerns
Duratorq 2.5 TDCi - late UN common-rail diesel, improved emissions but complex fuel system
foundIn: UF generation (1985-1999) - WL, R2 diesel engines
UN generation (1998-2006) - MD25, Duratorq TDCi diesel engines
knownFor: Strong low-end torque ideal for towing and loaded commercial use
WL injector pump wear between 90k-120k miles on high-mileage work trucks
Turbocharger bearing failure on WL-T units after 100k miles
High UK replacement demand on B2500/B2600 commercial variants
items.cta: Get quotes for Mazda B Series Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Mazda B Series Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol engines offered simpler, carburetted or early EFI reliability for light commercial and private B Series use, with the F2 and G6 families covering most variants.
items.descriptor: Petrol engines offered simpler, carburetted or early EFI reliability for light commercial and private B Series use, with the F2 and G6 families covering most variants.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mazda B Series Petrol Engine Families
families: F2 - 2.2L workhorse petrol (B2200), carburettor and valve seal wear issues
FE - 2.0L economy petrol (early B2200), timing belt tensioner concerns
G6 - 2.6L performance petrol (B2600), oil pump and exhaust manifold issues
JE - 3.0L V6 refinement (B3000), timing chain and intake gasket wear
foundIn: UF generation (1985-1999) - F2, FE, G6 petrol engines
UN generation (1998-2006) - JE, Duratec petrol engines
knownFor: Simpler mechanical design easier for independent specialists to repair
Carburettor jet clogging on early non-EFI F2 units stored long-term
Timing belt critical on all belt-driven petrol engines; interference design
Lower replacement demand than diesel, but parts availability can be limited
items.cta: Compare Mazda B Series Petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Mazda B Series Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Mazda B Series production timeline (1985-2006), organised so commercial vehicle owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: UF Launch: R2 & Early WL Diesel
years.description: The original UF-generation B Series launched with basic R2 diesel and F2 petrol engines, establishing the pickup's reputation for simple, repairable commercial reliability.
keyChanges: UF platform introduced; rear-wheel drive commercial pickup architecture
R2 2.2L diesel and F2 2.2L petrol engines standard on B2200 variants
Single cab and double cab body styles available from launch
knownFor: Simple, mechanically straightforward engines easy for independent specialists to repair
Carburettor jet clogging on F2 petrol units stored long-term
Early WL variants introduced late in this period for B2500
years.cta: Get quotes for 1985-1990 Mazda B Series replacement engines

years.description: The WL 2.5L diesel became the backbone of the B Series range, powering the popular B2500 and B2600 with proven torque for UK commercial and agricultural use.
keyChanges: WL 2.5L diesel introduced across B2500/B2600 variants
WL-T turbo diesel option added for higher torque applications
G6 2.6L petrol introduced for performance-oriented B2600 variants
mainEngines: WL-T - 2.5L turbo diesel (B2500 Turbo, B2600 Turbo)
knownFor: WL injector pump wear emerging as common high-mileage failure point
Turbocharger bearing issues on WL-T units after 100k miles
Strong commercial demand keeping these variants active in UK used market
years.cta: Get quotes for 1990-1998 Mazda B Series replacement engines
years.ticker: Mazda B Series WL injector pump replacement • Mazda B Series WL-T turbo failure • Mazda B2500 engine replacement cost
enquiries: Mazda B Series WL injector pump replacement
Mazda B Series WL-T turbo failure

years.description: The UN-generation B Series shared architecture with the Ford Ranger, introducing MD25 diesel and JE V6 petrol engines with improved emissions compliance for post-2000 MoT requirements.
keyChanges: UN platform launched, sharing components with Ford Ranger
MD25 diesel replaced WL on many variants; Duratorq TDCi introduced late
JE 3.0L V6 petrol added for B3000; Duratec 4.0L for B4000
Emissions compliance updates for post-2000 MoT testing
mainEngines: MD25 - 2.5L diesel, Ford-derived (B2500 UN, B2600 UN)
MD25-T - 2.5L turbo diesel (B2500 Turbo UN, B2600 Turbo UN)
knownFor: MD25 head gasket failure on high-mileage commercial units
Timing belt critical on all belt-driven UN engines; interference design
Duratorq TDCi complexity requiring specialist diagnostic equipment
Higher parts commonality with Ford Ranger simplifying some repairs
years.cta: Get quotes for 1998-2006 Mazda B Series replacement engines
years.ticker: Mazda B Series MD25 head gasket • Mazda B Series Duratorq TDCi replacement • Mazda B3000 JE engine cost
enquiries: Mazda B Series MD25 head gasket

faq.h2: Mazda B Series Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mazda B Series engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A rebuilt Mazda B Series engine typically costs £650-£1,650 supply-only, depending on engine code (WL, MD25, F2 etc.) and condition. Supply & fit adds £800-£1,200 labour at UK commercial garages.
keyPoints: WL diesel rebuilt engines: £650-£1,450 supply-only
Turbo diesel (WL-T/MD25-T): £850-£1,650 supply-only
Petrol engines (F2/G6): £550-£1,250 supply-only
Supply & fit labour typically adds £800-£1,200
All rebuilt units include minimum 12-month warranty
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Mazda B Series engine prices for your variant

items.question: What engine codes fit my Mazda B Series?
items.answer: Common Mazda B Series engine codes include WL, WL-T, MD25 (diesel) and F2, FE, G6, JE (petrol). Enter your registration above to verify the exact code fitted to your vehicle.
keyPoints: Diesel: WL, WL-T, WL-TC, MD25, MD25-T, Duratorq 2.5 TDCi
Petrol: F2, FE, G6, JE, Duratec 4.0
UF generation (1985-1999): WL, F2, FE, G6
UN generation (1998-2006): MD25, JE, Duratorq
Always verify code before ordering replacement
items.cta: Use our engine code checker for your Mazda B Series

items.question: Are Mazda B Series WL diesel engines reliable?
items.answer: The WL diesel is generally robust for commercial use, but injector pump wear and turbocharger failure are documented issues on high-mileage B2500/B2600 units over 90k miles.
keyPoints: WL engines known for strong low-end torque and simplicity
Injector pump wear common after 90k-120k miles
Turbocharger bearing failure on WL-T variants after 100k miles
Regular servicing extends life significantly
Rebuilt units address known failure points with upgraded components
items.cta: Get quotes for reliable rebuilt WL engines

items.question: Can I swap a different engine into my Mazda B Series?
items.answer: Engine swaps are possible but complex; WL and MD25 blocks share mounting points, but ECU, wiring harness and emissions compliance must be verified for your specific variant and year.
keyPoints: WL and MD25 share physical mounting on many chassis
ECU and immobiliser pairing required for UN-generation swaps
Emissions compliance critical for post-2000 MoT testing
Professional fitting recommended to preserve warranty
Always confirm compatibility with supplier before purchase
items.cta: Enquire about compatible engine swaps for your B Series

items.question: How long does Mazda B Series engine fitting take?
items.answer: Most Mazda B Series engine replacements take 1-2 working days at a specialist commercial garage, with many suppliers offering next-day engine dispatch for urgent commercial vehicle repairs.
keyPoints: Engine supply typically 24-48 hours from UK stock
Professional fitting: 1-2 days depending on garage workload
Supply & fit packages available nationwide
Urgent dispatch options for commercial fleet operators
Book in advance during peak agricultural/commercial seasons
items.cta: Find Mazda B Series supply & fit deals near you

items.question: What warranty comes with Mazda B Series replacement engines?
items.answer: All rebuilt and reconditioned Mazda B Series engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty; used engine warranties vary by supplier.
keyPoints: Rebuilt/recon engines: 12-month unlimited mileage standard
Used engines: typically 3-6 months, supplier-dependent
Warranty covers parts and labour at fitting garage
Commercial use coverage confirmed with supplier
Extended warranty options available on premium rebuilds
items.cta: View Mazda B Series engine warranty options

items.question: Do I need to replace the ECU with my Mazda B Series engine?
items.answer: Most suppliers provide matched ECU and wiring harness with rebuilt UN-generation engines; UF-generation engines often retain original ECU if immobiliser is unchanged.
keyPoints: UF engines (pre-1999): often retain original ECU
UN engines (1998+): ECU/harness typically supplied matched
Immobiliser pairing required for post-1998 variants
Confirm ECU inclusion with supplier before ordering
Professional fitting ensures correct integration
items.cta: Check ECU compatibility for your Mazda B Series engine

items.question: What's the difference between recon and used Mazda B Series engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned engines are professionally repaired with worn parts replaced and come with warranty; used engines are second-hand donor units with variable condition and shorter/no warranty.
keyPoints: Recon: inspected, repaired, tested, 12-month warranty
Used: as-removed from donor, variable mileage/condition
Recon addresses known failure points; used may inherit them
Used cheaper upfront but higher long-term risk
Recon recommended for commercial vehicle reliability
items.cta: Compare recon vs used Mazda B Series engine prices

items.question: How do I find my Mazda B Series engine code?
items.answer: Your Mazda B Series engine code is stamped on the engine block (typically near the injector pump or timing cover) and recorded on your V5C logbook under "engine number".
keyPoints: Check V5C logbook: "engine number" field contains code
Physical stamp on engine block near injector pump/timing cover
Common codes: WL, MD25 (diesel); F2, G6, JE (petrol)
Enter registration above for instant DVLA verification
Supplier can confirm compatibility once code is known
items.cta: Verify your Mazda B Series engine code instantly

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on an older Mazda B Series?
items.answer: For a B2500/B2600 in good structural condition, a rebuilt engine (£650-£1,450) often offers better value than purchasing another used pickup, especially for commercial users needing reliability.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine cost: £650-£1,450 + £800-£1,200 fit
Comparable used B2500: £2,000-£4,000+ with unknown history
Rebuilt engine includes warranty and addressed failure points
Consider chassis condition, MoT status and commercial use needs
Get quotes for both options before deciding
items.cta: Compare engine replacement vs buying another B Series
